answer: The MCP corpus for Dunoyer's Nouveau traité d'économie Vol I contains chapters with Roman numerals I (race), II (external circumstances), and V (culture) — sections III and IV are absent. There is no chapter explicitly titled 'Influence of climate on liberty' (which would be section III of the original work) in the corpus. The closest chapter is Chapter 3 (work_slug: charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce, chapter_number: 3), titled 'II. Influence des circonstances extérieures sur la liberté' (Influence of external circumstances on liberty), which contains the most extensive discussion of climate's influence on liberty in the corpus — covering Montesquieu, Cabanis, and Comte's views on how climate and geographical circumstances shape the development and freedom of peoples. If the question refers to a chapter specifically titled on climate (section III of the original), that chapter is not present in the corpus.
